restaurant_name_en: Jing Xi Hui (Amazing Chinese Cuisine — The Bund)
restaurant_group: 菁禧荟 (Amazing Chinese Cuisine — has multiple high-end branches; Bund is the Shanghai flagship)
vibe_and_space: Formal Cantonese fine-dining register on the Bund — premium private rooms, refined tableware, full sommelier and tea service. Comparable in tier to Imperial Treasure or House of Rong.
one_paragraph_recommendation: Shanghai's most decorated Chaoshan / Cantonese fine-dining destination — Black Pearl 三钻 for seven consecutive years (one of just six 三钻 restaurants nationally in 2026). Recommend for a high-stakes business dinner where you need an institutional signal, want 潮汕 ingredient-driven precision rather than grand-banquet Cantonese pageantry, and don't care about price. The 卤水 and 刺身 sections are the discriminators vs. House of Rong / Imperial Treasure (which lean Hong Kong / Guangzhou banquet). Not the place for a casual evening or for diners who don't already speak the 潮汕 vocabulary.
